title: Active layers and optoelectronic devices based on non-fullerenes and/or hole scavengers
abstract: Embodiments of the present disclosure describe an active layer of an optoelectronic device comprising a non-fullerene component and optionally one or more hole-scavenging components. Embodiments of the present disclosure describe an optoelectronic device comprising a first electrode material, an active layer, and a second electrode material, wherein the first electrode material and the second electrode material are on opposing sides of the active layer, wherein the active layer comprises a non-fullerene component and optionally one or more hole-scavenging components.
claims:
Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. An active layer of an optoelectronic device comprising one or more non- fullerene components and one or more hole-scavenging components. 2. The layer according to claim 1, wherein a ratio of the one or more hole scavenging components to the one or more non-fullerene components ranges from about 1: 10 to about 1 :25. 3. The layer according to any one of claims 1-2, wherein the one or more hole scavenging components comprise a first hole-scavenging component and a second hole scavenging component, wherein the first hole scavenging component and the second hole scavenging component are different. 4. The layer according to any one of claims 1-3, wherein the non-fullerene component includes one or more of rhodanine-benzothiadiazole-coupled indacenodithiophene (IDTBR); indacenodithieno[3,2-b]thiophene, IT), end-capped with 2- (3-oxo-2,3-dihydroinden-l-ylidene)malononitrile (INCN) groups (ITIC); indaceno[l,2- b:5,6-b']dithiophene and 2-(3-oxo-2,3-dihydroinden-l-ylidene)malononitrile (IEIC); 2,2'- ((2Z,2'Z)-((5,5'-(4,4,9,9-tetrakis(4-hexylphenyl)-4,9-dihydro-s-indaceno[l,2-b:5,6- b'] dithiophene-2, 7-diyl)bis(4-((2-ethylhexyl)- oxy)thiophene-5,2- diyl))bis(methanylylidene))bis(3-oxo-2,3-di- hydro- lH-indene-2,l- diylidene))dimalononitrile (IEICO); naphthalene diimide (NDI); bay-linked perylene bisimide (di-PBI); perylene bisimide (PBI); Benzotriazole-Containing End-Capped with Thiazolidine-2,4-dione (TD); Naphthalocyanine (NC); Phthalocyanine (PC); Naphtho[l,2- c :5,6- c ']bis[l,2,5]thiadiazole; (2E,2'E)-3,3'-(2,5-dimethoxy-l,4-phenylene)bis(2-(5-(4- (N-(2-ethylhexyl)- 1 ,8-naphthalimide)yl)thiophen-2-yl)acrylonitrile) (NIDCS-MO); thieno[3,4-b] thiophene and 2-( 1 , 1 -dicyanomethylene)rhodanine combination (ATT-l); (3,9-bis(4-(l,l-dicyanomethylene)- 3-methylene-2-oxo-cyclopenta[b]thiophen)-5,5,l 1,11- tetrakis(4-hexylphenyl)-dithieno[2,3-d':2,3-d']-s-indaceno[l,2-b:5,6- b']-di thiophene 

 (ITCC); Indanedione; Dicyannovinyl; Benzothiadiazole; Diketopyrolopyrrole; arylene diimide; and IDIC. 5. The layer according to any one of claims 1-4, wherein the one or more hole scavenging components include one or more of thiophene, acene, fluorine, carbazole, indacenodithieno thiophene, indacenothieno thiphene, benzodithiazole, thieny- benzodithiophene-dione, benzotriazole, and diketopyrrolopyrrole. 6. The layer according to any one of claims 1-5, wherein the active layer is deposited on a transparent substrate coated with either indium tin oxide or fluorine-doped tin oxide. 7. The layer according to any one of claims 1-6, wherein the optoelectronic device is one or more of an organic photovoltaic, organic photodiode, organic light-emitting diode, organic photo field-effect transistor, photodetector, and paraelectric. 8. An optoelectronic device, comprising: a first electrode material; an active layer; and a second electrode material; wherein the first electrode material and the second electrode material are on opposing sides of the active layer, wherein the active layer comprises one or more non-fullerene components and one or more hole-scavenging components. 9. The device according to claim 8, wherein a ratio of the one or more hole scavenging components to the one or more non-fullerene components ranges from about 1: 10 to about 1 :25. 10. The device according to any one of claims 8-9, wherein the one or more hole scavenging components comprise a first hole-scavenging component and a second hole- 

 scavenging component, wherein the first hole scavenging component and the second hole scavenging component are different. 11. The device according to any one of claims 8-10, wherein the non-fullerene component includes one or more of includes one or more of rhodanine-benzothiadiazole- coupled indacenodithiophene (IDTBR); indacenodithieno[3,2-b]thiophene, IT), end-capped with 2-(3-oxo-2,3-dihydroinden-l-ylidene)malononitrile (INCN) groups (ITIC); indaceno[l,2-b:5,6-b']dithiophene and 2-(3-oxo-2,3-dihydroinden-l-ylidene)malononitrile (IEIC); 2,2'-((2Z,2'Z)-((5,5'-(4,4,9,9-tetrakis(4-hexylphenyl)-4,9-dihydro-s-indaceno[l,2- b:5,6-b']dithiophene-2,7-diyl)bis(4-((2-ethylhexyl)- oxy)thiophene-5,2- diyl))bis(methanylylidene))bis(3-oxo-2,3-di- hydro- lH-indene-2,l- diylidene))dimalononitrile (IEICO); naphthalene diimide (NDI); bay-linked perylene bisimide (di-PBI); perylene bisimide (PBI); Benzotriazole-Containing End-Capped with Thiazolidine-2,4-dione (TD); Naphthalocyanine (NC); Phthalocyanine (PC); Naphtho[l,2- c :5,6- c ']bis[l,2,5]thiadiazole; (2E,2'E)-3,3'-(2,5-dimethoxy-l,4-phenylene)bis(2-(5-(4- (N-(2-ethylhexyl)- 1 ,8-naphthalimide)yl)thiophen-2-yl)acrylonitrile) (NIDCS-MO); thieno[3,4-b] thiophene and 2-( 1 , 1 -dicyanomethylene)rhodanine combination (ATT-l); (3,9-bis(4-(l,l-dicyanomethylene)- 3-methylene-2-oxo-cyclopenta[b]thiophen)-5,5,l 1,11- tetrakis(4-hexylphenyl)-dithieno[2,3-d':2,3-d']-s-indaceno[l,2-b:5,6- b']-di thiophene (ITCC); Indanedione; Dicyannovinyl; Benzothiadiazole; Diketopyrolopyrrole; arylene diimide; and IDIC. 12. The device according to any one of claims 8-11, wherein the hole scavenging component includes one or more of thiophene, acene, fluorine, carbazole, indacenodithieno thiophene, indacenothieno thiphene, benzodithiazole, thieny-benzodithiophene-dione, benzotriazole, and diketopyrrolopyrrole. 13. The device according to any one of claims 8-12, wherein the first electrode material and/or second electrode material is one or more of a doped oxide, metallic conductor, conducting polymer, and carbon-based conductor. 14. The device according to claim 13, wherein the doped oxide is one or more of indium-doped tin oxide (ITO), fluoride-doped tin oxide (FTO), aluminum-doped zinc oxide (AZO), and In 2 0 3 . 15. The device according to any one of claims 8-14, further comprising a transparent substrate, a first selective contact layer, and a second selective contact layer. 16. The device according to claim 15, wherein the first selective contact layer and/or second selective contact layer include one or more of PEDOT:PSS, nickel oxide, graphene, fluorine-doped CsSnh, perovskites, metal-phthalocynanine (e.g., copper- phthalocyanine), Cul, PFN, metal-thiocyanate, phthalocyanine, polyacetylene, poly(phenylene vinylene), bathocuproine, and derivatives thereof. 17. A tandem solar cell, comprising: a substrate; a first subcell including at least an active layer, wherein the active layer comprises a non-fullerene component and one or more hole-scavenging components; and a second subcell selected from amorphous silicon, perovskite crystal structures, solid-state dye sensitized structures, liquid-electrolyte dye sensitized structures, cadium telluride materials, cadmium sulfide materials, and gallium arsenide materials; wherein the first subcell and the substrate are on opposing sides of the second subcell. 18. The solar cell according to claim 17, wherein the first subcell absorbs at least a portion of light in the near-infrared region of the electromagnetic spectrum. 19. The solar cell according to any one of claims 17-18, wherein the second subcell absorbs at least a portion of light in the visible region of the electromagnetic spectrum. 20. The solar cell according to any one of claims 17-19, wherein the one or more hole-scavenging components comprise a first hole scavenging component and a second hole scavenging component, wherein the first hole scavenging component and the second hole scavenging component are different.
